title: [Bug]: Add support for "buildx build --push" and/or "buildx build --output=type=registry"
body:
### Issue Description

I am using [DevSpace](https://github.com/devspace-sh/devspace) CLI tool which in some cases calls buildkit via CLI, and it relies on the `--push` flag of the `docker buildx build` command. So far this is the only compatibility problem that I found when using podman(with podman-docker).
According to [the buildx reference](https://github.com/docker/buildx/blob/master/docs/reference/buildx_build.md#options), the `--push` flag is just shorthand for `--output=type=registry`. So IMHO implementing both these things would be ideal, but I will be happy with either.
I may be able to contribute a PR if you think this is not to complex, and can give me a couple of pointers in the right direction.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
A:
1. Install podman and podman-docker
2. Run `docker buildx build --tag test --push --file Dockerfile`
B:
1. Install podman and podman-docker
2. Run `docker buildx build --tag quay.io/YOUR_ORG/YOUR_REPO:podman --file Dockerfile --output=type=registry`

### Describe the results you received

A:
```
Error: unknown flag: --push
See 'podman buildx build --help'
```
B: Image is built successfully(exit code 0), but it is not pushed to the registry.



### Describe the results you expected

In both cases I expect image to be built and pushed to the registry.
